summary: Testing new 'Formalités et Documents Nécessaires' feature. Backend API working correctly, returns authentic tariff data with formalities. CRITICAL BUG: Frontend React error in <RegimeBreakdown> component inside DetailedCalculationBreakdown.jsx prevents results from rendering. The component expects different data structure than what authentic tariff API provides.

critical.issue: React error when rendering with authentic tariff data - data structure mismatch. Component expects tax_lines, fob_value, freight, insurance but authentic API returns dd, vat, other_taxes structure
critical.error_message: An error occurred in the <RegimeBreakdown> component

integration_issues.issue: Results section fails to render due to RegimeBreakdown component error. API call succeeds (console shows '✅ Using AUTHENTIC tariff data for SEN') but React component tree crashes

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: RegimeBreakdown component data structure mismatch with authentic tariff API
Option A: Adapt authentic API response to match expected structure (tax_lines format) before passing to DetailedCalculationBreakdown
Option B: Check data_source and skip DetailedCalculationBreakdown for authentic_tariff data since main results UI already handles it correctly
Option C: Create separate DetailedCalculationBreakdown variant for authentic tariff data
Add Error Boundary around DetailedCalculationBreakdown to prevent full component tree crash
critical_code_review_comments: DetailedCalculationBreakdown.jsx expects result.npf_calculation.tax_lines, result.npf_calculation.fob_value etc but authentic API returns result.npf_calculation.dd, result.npf_calculation.vat structure
Main results section in CalculatorTab (lines 656-1161) correctly handles authentic data with data_source === 'authentic_tariff' checks
The formalities section code (lines 960-1161) is correctly implemented with all required documents but won't render due to React crash
No error boundary exists in the app to gracefully handle component errors

success_rate.backend: 100% - API /api/authentic-tariffs/calculate working correctly, returns formalities, taxes_detail, fiscal_advantages
success_rate.frontend: 0% - Results fail to render due to RegimeBreakdown component crash

context_for_next_testing_agent: The formalities section code EXISTS and is correctly implemented (lines 960-1161 in CalculatorTab.jsx). Backend API works correctly and returns administrative_formalities, fiscal_advantages, taxes_detail. However, a bug in DetailedCalculationBreakdown.jsx (specifically the RegimeBreakdown sub-component) causes React error that prevents entire results section from rendering. Main agent needs to fix data structure mismatch between authentic tariff API response and what DetailedCalculationBreakdown expects.

backend_api.details: GET /api/authentic-tariffs/calculate/SEN/870323?value=100000&language=fr returns correct data with administrative_formalities, taxes_detail, fiscal_advantages

formalities_section_code.details: Code exists at lines 960-1161, includes: Facture Commerciale, Liste de Colisage, Connaissement, Déclaration en Douane, Certificat d'Origine ZLECAf, Déclaration du Fournisseur

formalities_section_rendering.details: Cannot verify rendering due to RegimeBreakdown component crash

select_components_fix.details: Select components working correctly - verified in screenshot showing Maroc and Sénégal selections

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Data structure mismatch between authentic tariff API response and DetailedCalculationBreakdown component expectations
reproduction_steps: 5. Observe: Console shows '✅ Using AUTHENTIC tariff data for SEN' but results don't render
6. Console shows React error in RegimeBreakdown component
rca_of_the_issue.technical_details: DetailedCalculationBreakdown receives authenticResult which has npf_calculation.dd, npf_calculation.vat structure but RegimeBreakdown tries to access calculation.tax_lines, calculation.fob_value which don't exist
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Either transform authentic API response to match expected format, or conditionally skip DetailedCalculationBreakdown for authentic_tariff data_source, or add error boundary
